questions.q: Who was the grieving king whose death is described in this episode?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was the king of Ayodhya whose death from grief over Rama's exile is the subject of this card.

questions.q: Of which kingdom was Dasharatha the king?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha ruled the kingdom of Ayodhya in the land of Kosala.

questions.q: Whose departure caused Dasharatha such overwhelming grief that he died?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha died unable to bear the loss of his beloved eldest son Rama, who had been exiled.

questions.q: Why did Rama leave Ayodhya, leading to Dasharatha's heartbreak?
options: He was banished to the forest for fourteen years
He left to study with sages
He was sent to rule a distant province
questions.explanation: Rama was sent into a fourteen-year forest exile to honor a boon Dasharatha had granted Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Which queen's demand forced Dasharatha to exile Rama?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi demanded Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation, using the two boons Dasharatha owed her.

questions.q: What did the dying Dasharatha keep calling out as he passed away?
questions.explanation: The old king died calling out the name of his son Rama, consumed by longing and grief.

questions.q: Who was Dasharatha's chief queen and the mother of Rama?
questions.explanation: Kausalya was Dasharatha's eldest queen and the mother of Rama.

questions.q: Whom did Kaikeyi want crowned king in place of Rama?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi used her boons to have her own son Bharata installed as king of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who accompanied Rama into the forest exile?
questions.explanation: Sita, Rama's wife, and Lakshmana, his devoted brother, chose to share his forest exile.

questions.q: Dasharatha's death is described as the fulfillment of what?
questions.explanation: His death is portrayed as a bereaved father's curse at last coming due upon him.

questions.q: Whose son had Dasharatha accidentally killed in his youth, earning a curse from the grieving parents?
questions.explanation: As a young hunter Dasharatha killed Shravana Kumara by mistake, and the boy's blind parents cursed him to die of grief for a son.

questions.q: What was the curse the blind parents of the slain youth laid upon Dasharatha?
options: That he would lose his kingdom
That he too would die grieving for a son
That he would never have children
That his sons would betray him
questions.explanation: The bereaved parents cursed Dasharatha that he, like them, would die in agony of separation from a son.

questions.q: By what skill had the young Dasharatha hunted, leading to the fatal accident?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha used shabda-bhedi, the art of shooting an arrow at a sound, and struck the youth mistaking the sound of his water pot for an animal.

questions.q: Where was Rama at the time of Dasharatha's death?
questions.explanation: Rama was already living in forest exile and was absent and unaware when his father died.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Dasharatha died?
options: Away at his maternal grandfather's kingdom
questions.explanation: Bharata was away visiting his maternal uncle's home in the Kekaya kingdom and had to be summoned back after the king's death.

questions.q: Who was the loyal charioteer and minister who drove Rama to the forest's edge and returned to a grieving Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra, Dasharatha's trusted charioteer, drove Rama out and returned heartbroken to the capital.

questions.q: How many boons had Dasharatha once promised Kaikeyi, which she used to exile Rama?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi held two long-owed boons, demanding Bharata's crowning with one and Rama's exile with the other.

questions.q: On what earlier occasion had Kaikeyi earned the two boons from Dasharatha?
options: By saving him in a battle against the asuras
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi had once saved Dasharatha's life on a battlefield against the demons, and in gratitude he granted her two boons to claim later.

questions.q: Which queen, mother of Lakshmana and Shatrughna, was Dasharatha's third wife?
questions.explanation: Sumitra was Dasharatha's third queen and the mother of the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Which queen was at Dasharatha's side, sharing his grief, during his final night?
questions.explanation: Kausalya, Rama's mother, remained with the dying king through his last night of sorrow.

questions.q: What is the name of the section (kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana in which Dasharatha's death occurs?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha's death is narrated in the Ayodhya Kanda, the book concerned with events in and around Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who was the royal preceptor and chief priest of Ayodhya who guided the kingdom after Dasharatha's death?
questions.explanation: Sage Vasishtha was the family preceptor of the Ikshvaku dynasty and oversaw the rites and counsel after the king died.

questions.q: On which night after Rama's departure did Dasharatha die, according to the tradition?
options: The second night, in the early hours before dawn
On the day Bharata was crowned
questions.explanation: Dasharatha died in grief on the night following Rama's departure, in the last watch before dawn, never seeing his son again.

questions.q: What was the name of the youth (Shravana) whose accidental killing brought the curse upon Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: The slain youth was Shravana Kumara, a devoted son fetching water for his aged blind parents.

questions.q: What sound did young Dasharatha mistake for an animal when he loosed the fatal arrow?
options: The gurgle of water being drawn into a pot
questions.explanation: He heard the gurgling of water as Shravana filled his pot at the river and, thinking it an elephant drinking, shot toward the sound.

questions.q: After being summoned back, what did Bharata do regarding the throne Kaikeyi had won for him?
options: He refused it and sought to bring Rama back
He divided the kingdom with Shatrughna
He fled into the forest himself
questions.explanation: Bharata was horrified by his mother's deed, refused the throne, and went to the forest to beg Rama to return and rule.

questions.q: Which dynasty, into which Dasharatha was born, traced its line from the Sun?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha belonged to the Ikshvaku or Suryavamsha, the Solar dynasty descended from Surya through Manu.

questions.q: Until the king's last rites could be performed, how was Dasharatha's body preserved in Ayodhya?
options: Kept immersed in a vessel of oil
questions.explanation: Because his sons were absent, the ministers preserved Dasharatha's body in a trough of oil until Bharata returned to conduct the funeral rites.

questions.q: When Rama finally learned of his father's death, what rite did he perform for Dasharatha in the forest?
questions.explanation: On hearing the news from Bharata, Rama performed the water-offering and funerary tarpana for his departed father in the wilderness.

questions.q: What course did Bharata adopt to govern Ayodhya in Rama's absence after the funeral?
options: He ruled as crowned king in his own name
He placed Rama's sandals on the throne and ruled as regent
He left the throne empty and disbanded the court
questions.explanation: Bharata enshrined Rama's sandals (paduka) upon the throne and ruled only as a regent in Rama's name, awaiting his return.
